accurate.why: All claims accurately reflect the bill text; the description of Parkinson's as 'a progressive brain disorder that causes unintended or uncontrollable movements' is correct real-world context.

states_purpose.why: Clearly identifies the purpose as directing HHS to address Parkinson's disease and related conditions.

changes_to_existing_law.why: Implies the act creates new requirements for HHS; could be more explicit about the Public Health Service Act amendment but the framing is acceptable.

effective_dates.why: Notes the 2035 sunset; omits interim deadlines (24 months, 18 months) but these are not the bill's primary features.

major_provisions_covered.why: Captures the National Parkinson's Project, national plan requirement, annual assessment, Advisory Council, and sunset provision at appropriate high level.

concise.why: Appropriately brief; captures the essence without unnecessary detail.

correct_entities.why: HHS correctly named; Advisory Council described accurately.

judged_via: claude-sonnet-4-6 via parallel Claude Code subagents (web/knowledge-verified context)
